revision.comment: Bot: remove [[Category:Sn crud]] (crud category cleanup) [[github:EmmaLeonhart/shintowiki-scripts/actions/runs/23984597483|manual triggered pipeline]]
revision.text:
<!--interwikis from wikidata-->[[en:Furogu Shrine]][[ja:風浪宮]][[ko:풍랑궁]][[simple:Furogu Shrine]]


{{Short description|Shinto shrine}}
{{Infobox religious building| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| native_name           = 風浪宮 (fuuro-gu)
| religious_affiliation = [[Shinto]]
| image                 = File:Furogu front shrine.jpg
| caption               = View from in front of Torii Gate
| deity                 = {{ill|Watatsumi|qid=Q1474187|1=Watatsumi}}
| prefecture= {{ill|Okawa|qid=Q385248|1=Ōkawa, Fukuoka}}
| coordinates= {{Coord|33.211222|N|130.386722|E|region:JP_type:landmark|display=inline,title}}
}}
The '''{{ill|Furogu Shrine|en|Furogu Shrine|qid=Q11282543|lt=Furogu Shrine|1=Furogu Shrine}}''' (Palace of Wind and Waves, 風浪宮) is a {{ill|Shinto shrine|en|Shinto shrine|qid=Q845945|lt=shrine|1=Shinto shrine}} located in {{ill|Ōkawa, Fukuoka|en|Ōkawa, Fukuoka|qid=Q385248|lt=Okawa|1=Ōkawa, Fukuoka}}, {{ill|Fukuoka Prefecture|en|Fukuoka Prefecture|qid=Q123258|lt=Fukuoka Prefecture|1=Fukuoka Prefecture}}.<ref name="archiveph">{{Cite web |title=Furogu Shrine |url=https://kankou.chikugolife.jp/en/kankou/foreign_detail/420 |url-status=dead |archive-url=https://archive.today/20230310224005/https://kankou.chikugolife.jp/en/kankou/foreign_detail/420 |archive-date=10 Mar 2023 |access-date=2023-04-03 |website=archive.ph}}</ref> It is a central shrine of the city.<ref name=":0">{{Cite web |title=The history of Okawa |url=https://tsukiita.jp/en/publics/index/11/ |access-date=2023-11-27 |website=NAKAMURA TSUKIITA INC. |language=en}}</ref> It has been traditionally served by the [[Azumi people]].

== Overview ==
The origins of Shrine date back over 1,800 years<ref name="archiveph" /> and it is linked to {{ill|Empress Jingū|en|Empress Jingū|qid=Q232803|lt=Empress Jingū|1=Empress Jingū}}.<ref>https://okawa-kk.com/wp-content/uploads/2021/07/観光案内パンフ.pdf</ref>

There are 22 Watatsumi shrines in the region that claim to have been founded by her after her campaign including {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine_(Kobe)|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|simple|4=Draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine_(Kobe)|qq=}}.<ref>{{Cite web |title=The Romance of Kobe |url=https://www.forgottenbooks.com/en/readbook/TheRomanceofKobe_10127101#12 |access-date=2023-11-24 |website=www.forgottenbooks.com}}</ref>{{rp|7}}

The shrine is locally known as "Ofurosan".<ref name="archiveph" />

Furogu Shrine's Reitaisai happens in early February.<ref name=":1">{{Cite web |title=Ofurosan Festival |url=http://www.fukuoka-now.com/en/event/ofurosan-festival/ |access-date=2023-04-03 |website=Fukuoka Now}}</ref><ref name=":0" /> It is a major festival in the {{ill|Chikugo Province|en|Chikugo Province|qid=Q864159|lt=Chikugo region|1=Chikugo Province}}. It is one of three big festivals, with the others at Kora Shrine and Suitengu Shrine in Kurume. The festival is also known as "Ofurosan." It draws over 150,000 visitors. The festival is known for its exciting events.<ref name=":1" /> During the festival people carry [[matsuri float]]s and chant.<ref name=":0" /> The shioi-mairi and goshinko processions are highlights. Another attraction is the [[yabusame]], which is horseback archery. This event is very popular. Around 300 {{ill|Yatai (food cart)|en|Yatai (food cart)|qid=Q2039612|lt=stalls|1=Yatai (food cart)}} are set up near the shrine. They sell festival food. This includes dried {{ill|Persimmon|en|Persimmon|qid=Q29526|lt=Persimmon|1=Persimmon}}s and other items.<ref name=":1" />

Ths shrine has a massive [[Shinboku]] Camphor tree on its premises<ref name="archiveph" /><ref name=":0" /><ref name="JET-Guide-2006" /> The tree itself is 1800 years old<ref name="JET-Guide-2006">[https://www.travisheermann.com/images/JETGuide2006.pdf JET Guide 2006]</ref><ref name=":0" />

The current main shrine was rebuilt by Kamakura no Yoritomo, the lord of Yanagawa Castle during the {{ill|Sengoku period|en|Sengoku period|qid=Q204023|lt=Sengoku period|1=Sengoku period}}, and the main shrine and stone five-story pagoda have been designated as Important Cultural Properties by the national government. {{Citation needed|date=April 2023}}

It has a seated wooden figure of the Buddhist [[Kannon|Goddess of Mercy]]<ref name="JET-Guide-2006"/> and a dramatic standing statue of the sea god.<ref name=":0" />

The current shrine was built in 1560.<ref name="JET-Guide-2006" />

== Cultural Properties ==
=== Important Cultural Property (National Designation) ===
Honden - Built in the late Muromachi period (1560).<ref name="JET-Guide-2006" />
* Main shrine - Built in the late Muromachi period (1560).<ref name="JET-Guide-2006" />
* Stone five-story pagoda - Early Muromachi Period (1355){{Citation needed|date=April 2023}}. Designated on May 27, 1907.<ref name="archiveph" />
It is inscribed with the date of the 10th month of the 10th year of the Shohei Era. Designated on August 29, 1910.{{Citation needed|date=April 2023}}

== See also ==
* {{ill|Watatsumi Shrine_(Kobe)|lt=Watatsumi Shrine|simple|4=Draft|12=simple|13=User:Immanuelle/Watatsumi Shrine_(Kobe)|qq=}}

== External links ==
* [http://www.ofurousan.jp/ Official website of Furogu Shrine]
